title: antidesma elassophyllum
intro: Antidesma elassophyllum, also known as the Filipino velvet apple tree, is a plant species native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bark of the plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, providing various health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving cough, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied, and more research is needed to confirm its benefits.
